content:
# Thermal Stability Test

**Summary**: Testing the stability of an emulsion under elevated temperatures to simulate reservoir conditions.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-mohamed|MOHAMED, 2014]] used thermal stability tests at 120°C to evaluate the performance of emulsified gels.

- [[2014-hashmat|Hashmat, 2014]] conducted thermal stability tests to ensure the polymer gels remain stable at high reservoir temperatures.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2014-hashmat|Hashmat, 2014]] conducted thermal stability tests to ensure the polymer gels remain stable at high reservoir temperatures.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Emulsions with smaller droplet sizes were found to be more stable during heating. ([[2014-mohamed|MOHAMED, 2014, p. 83]])

- The HPAM/PEI (3/1.2) system showed a 2935% increase in storage modulus when aged at 120°C compared to its base polymer. ([[2014-hashmat|Hashmat, 2014, p. 58]])
